column4: And it shall come to pass, because ye hearken to these ordinances, and keep, and do them, that the LORD thy God shall keep with thee the covenant and the mercy which {{c1::He}} swore unto thy fathers,

And it shall come to pass, {{c1::because}} ye hearken to these ordinances, and keep, and do them, that the LORD thy God shall keep with thee the covenant and the mercy which He swore unto thy fathers,

and {{c1::He}} will love thee, and bless thee, and multiply thee; He will also bless the fruit of thy body and the fruit of thy land, thy corn and thy wine and thine oil, the increase of thy kine and the young of thy flock, in the land which He swore unto thy fathers to give thee.

and He will love thee, and bless thee, and {{c1::multiply}} thee; He will also bless the fruit of thy body and the fruit of thy land, thy corn and thy wine and thine oil, the increase of thy kine and the young of thy flock, in the land which He swore unto thy fathers to give thee.
Thou shalt be {{c1::blessed}} above all peoples; there shall not be male or female barren among you, or among your cattle.
Thou shalt be blessed above all {{c1::peoples}}; there shall not be male or female barren among you, or among your cattle.
And the LORD will take away from thee all sickness; and {{c1::He}} will put none of the evil diseases of Egypt, which thou knowest, upon thee, but will lay them upon all them that hate thee.
And the LORD will take away from thee all sickness; and He will put none of the evil diseases of {{c1::Egypt}}, which thou knowest, upon thee, but will lay them upon all them that hate thee.
And thou shalt {{c1::consume}} all the peoples that the LORD thy God shall deliver unto thee; thine eye shall not pity them; neither shalt thou serve their gods; for that will be a snare unto thee.
And thou shalt consume all the {{c1::peoples}} that the LORD thy God shall deliver unto thee; thine eye shall not pity them; neither shalt thou serve their gods; for that will be a snare unto thee.
If thou shalt say in thy heart: ‘{{c1::These}} nations are more than I; how can I dispossess them?’
If thou shalt say in thy heart: ‘These nations are more than {{c1::I}}; how can I dispossess them?’
thou shalt not be afraid of them; thou shalt well remember what the LORD thy God did unto {{c1::Pharaoh}}, and unto all Egypt:
thou shalt not be afraid of them; thou shalt well remember what the LORD thy God did unto Pharaoh, and unto all {{c1::Egypt}}:

the great {{c1::trials}} which thine eyes saw, and the signs, and the wonders, and the mighty hand, and the outstretched arm, whereby the LORD thy God brought thee out; so shall the LORD thy God do unto all the peoples of whom thou art afraid.

the great trials which thine eyes saw, and the signs, and the {{c1::wonders}}, and the mighty hand, and the outstretched arm, whereby the LORD thy God brought thee out; so shall the LORD thy God do unto all the peoples of whom thou art afraid.
{{c1::Moreover}} the LORD thy God will send the hornet among them, until they that are left, and they that hide themselves, perish from before thee. .
Moreover the LORD thy God will send the {{c1::hornet}} among them, until they that are left, and they that hide themselves, perish from before thee. .
Thou shalt not be {{c1::affrighted}} at them; for the LORD thy God is in the midst of thee, a God great and awful.
And the LORD thy God will cast out those {{c1::nations}} before thee by little and little; thou mayest not consume them quickly, lest the beasts of the field increase upon thee.
And the LORD thy God will cast out those nations {{c1::before}} thee by little and little; thou mayest not consume them quickly, lest the beasts of the field increase upon thee.
But the LORD thy God shall {{c1::deliver}} them up before thee, and shall discomfit them with a great discomfiture, until they be destroyed.
But the LORD thy God shall deliver them up {{c1::before}} thee, and shall discomfit them with a great discomfiture, until they be destroyed.
And {{c1::He}} shall deliver their kings into thy hand, and thou shalt make their name to perish from under heaven; there shall no man be able to stand against thee, until thou have destroyed them.
And He shall {{c1::deliver}} their kings into thy hand, and thou shalt make their name to perish from under heaven; there shall no man be able to stand against thee, until thou have destroyed them.

The {{c1::graven}} images of their gods shall ye burn with fire; thou shalt not covet the silver or the gold that is on them, nor take it unto thee, lest thou be snared therein; for it is an abomination to the LORD thy God.

The graven {{c1::images}} of their gods shall ye burn with fire; thou shalt not covet the silver or the gold that is on them, nor take it unto thee, lest thou be snared therein; for it is an abomination to the LORD thy God.
And thou shalt not bring an {{c1::abomination}} into thy house, and be accursed like unto it; thou shalt utterly detest it, and thou shalt utterly abhor it; for it is a devoted thing.
And thou shalt not bring an abomination into thy house, and be {{c1::accursed}} like unto it; thou shalt utterly detest it, and thou shalt utterly abhor it; for it is a devoted thing.
All the commandment which {{c1::I}} command thee this day shall ye observe to do, that ye may live, and multiply, and go in and possess the land which the LORD swore unto your fathers.
All the {{c1::commandment}} which I command thee this day shall ye observe to do, that ye may live, and multiply, and go in and possess the land which the LORD swore unto your fathers.

And thou shalt remember all the way which the LORD thy God hath led thee these forty years in the wilderness, that {{c1::He}} might afflict thee, to prove thee, to know what was in thy heart, whether thou wouldest keep His commandments, or no.

And thou shalt {{c1::remember}} all the way which the LORD thy God hath led thee these forty years in the wilderness, that He might afflict thee, to prove thee, to know what was in thy heart, whether thou wouldest keep His commandments, or no.

And {{c1::He}} afflicted thee, and suffered thee to hunger, and fed thee with manna, which thou knewest not, neither did thy fathers know; that He might make thee know that man doth not live by bread only, but by every thing that proceedeth out of the mouth of the LORD doth man live.

And He {{c1::afflicted}} thee, and suffered thee to hunger, and fed thee with manna, which thou knewest not, neither did thy fathers know; that He might make thee know that man doth not live by bread only, but by every thing that proceedeth out of the mouth of the LORD doth man live.
Thy {{c1::raiment}} waxed not old upon thee, neither did thy foot swell, these forty years.
Thy raiment waxed not old upon thee, {{c1::neither}} did thy foot swell, these forty years.
And thou shalt {{c1::consider}} in thy heart, that, as a man chasteneth his son, so the LORD thy God chasteneth thee.
And thou shalt consider in thy heart, that, as a man {{c1::chasteneth}} his son, so the LORD thy God chasteneth thee.
And thou shalt keep the {{c1::commandments}} of the LORD thy God, to walk in His ways, and to fear Him.
For the LORD thy God {{c1::bringeth}} thee into a good land, a land of brooks of water, of fountains and depths, springing forth in valleys and hills;
For the LORD thy God bringeth thee into a good land, a land of {{c1::brooks}} of water, of fountains and depths, springing forth in valleys and hills;
a land of wheat and {{c1::barley}}, and vines and fig-trees and pomegranates; a land of olive-trees and honey;
a land of wheat and barley, and vines and fig-trees and {{c1::pomegranates}}; a land of olive-trees and honey;
a land {{c1::wherein}} thou shalt eat bread without scarceness, thou shalt not lack any thing in it; a land whose stones are iron, and out of whose hills thou mayest dig brass.
a land wherein thou shalt eat bread {{c1::without}} scarceness, thou shalt not lack any thing in it; a land whose stones are iron, and out of whose hills thou mayest dig brass.
And thou shalt eat and be satisfied, and bless the LORD thy God for the good land which {{c1::He}} hath given thee.
And thou shalt eat and be {{c1::satisfied}}, and bless the LORD thy God for the good land which He hath given thee.
Beware lest thou forget the LORD thy God, in not keeping His commandments, and His ordinances, and His statutes, which {{c1::I}} command thee this day;
{{c1::Beware}} lest thou forget the LORD thy God, in not keeping His commandments, and His ordinances, and His statutes, which I command thee this day;
lest when thou hast eaten and art {{c1::satisfied}}, and hast built goodly houses, and dwelt therein;
lest when thou hast eaten and art satisfied, and hast built {{c1::goodly}} houses, and dwelt therein;
and when thy herds and thy {{c1::flocks}} multiply, and thy silver and thy gold is multiplied, and all that thou hast is multiplied;
and when thy herds and thy flocks {{c1::multiply}}, and thy silver and thy gold is multiplied, and all that thou hast is multiplied;
then thy heart be lifted up, and thou forget the LORD thy God, who brought thee forth out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, out of the house of bondage;
then thy heart be {{c1::lifted}} up, and thou forget the LORD thy God, who brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage;

who led thee {{c1::through}} the great and dreadful wilderness, wherein were serpents, fiery serpents, and scorpions, and thirsty ground where was no water; who brought thee forth water out of the rock of flint;

who led thee through the great and {{c1::dreadful}} wilderness, wherein were serpents, fiery serpents, and scorpions, and thirsty ground where was no water; who brought thee forth water out of the rock of flint;
who fed thee in the wilderness with manna, which thy fathers knew not, that {{c1::He}} might afflict thee, and that He might prove thee, to do thee good at thy latter end;
who fed thee in the {{c1::wilderness}} with manna, which thy fathers knew not, that He might afflict thee, and that He might prove thee, to do thee good at thy latter end;
and thou say in thy heart: ‘{{c1::My}} power and the might of my hand hath gotten me this wealth.’
and thou say in thy heart: ‘My power and the might of my hand hath {{c1::gotten}} me this wealth.’
But thou shalt remember the LORD thy God, for it is {{c1::He}} that giveth thee power to get wealth, that He may establish His covenant which He swore unto thy fathers, as it is this day.
But thou shalt {{c1::remember}} the LORD thy God, for it is He that giveth thee power to get wealth, that He may establish His covenant which He swore unto thy fathers, as it is this day.
And it shall be, if thou shalt forget the LORD thy God, and walk after other gods, and serve them, and worship them, {{c1::I}} forewarn you this day that ye shall surely perish.
And it shall be, if thou shalt {{c1::forget}} the LORD thy God, and walk after other gods, and serve them, and worship them, I forewarn you this day that ye shall surely perish.
As the {{c1::nations}} that the LORD maketh to perish before you, so shall ye perish; because ye would not hearken unto the voice of the LORD your God.
As the nations that the LORD {{c1::maketh}} to perish before you, so shall ye perish; because ye would not hearken unto the voice of the LORD your God.
Hear, {{c1::O}} Israel: thou art to pass over the Jordan this day, to go in to dispossess nations greater and mightier than thyself, cities great and fortified up to heaven,
Hear, O {{c1::Israel}}: thou art to pass over the Jordan this day, to go in to dispossess nations greater and mightier than thyself, cities great and fortified up to heaven,
a people great and tall, the sons of the {{c1::Anakim}}, whom thou knowest, and of whom thou hast heard say: ‘Who can stand before the sons of Anak?’
a people great and tall, the sons of the Anakim, whom thou knowest, and of whom thou hast heard say: ‘{{c1::Who}} can stand before the sons of Anak?’

Know therefore this day, that the LORD thy God is {{c1::He}} who goeth over before thee as a devouring fire; He will destroy them, and He will bring them down before thee; so shalt thou drive them out, and make them to perish quickly, as the LORD hath spoken unto thee.

Know {{c1::therefore}} this day, that the LORD thy God is He who goeth over before thee as a devouring fire; He will destroy them, and He will bring them down before thee; so shalt thou drive them out, and make them to perish quickly, as the LORD hath spoken unto thee.

Speak not thou in thy heart, after that the LORD thy God hath {{c1::thrust}} them out from before thee, saying: ‘For my righteousness the LORD hath brought me in to possess this land’; whereas for the wickedness of these nations the LORD doth drive them out from before thee.

Speak not thou in thy heart, after that the LORD thy God hath thrust them out from {{c1::before}} thee, saying: ‘For my righteousness the LORD hath brought me in to possess this land’; whereas for the wickedness of these nations the LORD doth drive them out from before thee.

Not for thy righteousness, or for the uprightness of thy heart, dost thou go in to possess their land; but for the wickedness of these nations the LORD thy God doth drive them out from before thee, and that {{c1::He}} may establish the word which the LORD swore unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

Not for thy righteousness, or for the uprightness of thy heart, dost thou go in to possess their land; but for the wickedness of these nations the LORD thy God doth drive them out from before thee, and that He may establish the word which the LORD swore unto thy fathers, to {{c1::Abraham}}, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
Know {{c1::therefore}} that it is not for thy righteousness that the LORD thy God giveth thee this good land to possess it; for thou art a stiffnecked people.
Know therefore that it is not for thy {{c1::righteousness}} that the LORD thy God giveth thee this good land to possess it; for thou art a stiffnecked people.

Remember, forget thou not, how thou didst make the LORD thy God wroth in the wilderness; from the day that thou didst go forth out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, until ye came unto this place, ye have been rebellious against the LORD.

{{c1::Remember}}, forget thou not, how thou didst make the LORD thy God wroth in the wilderness; from the day that thou didst go forth out of the land of Egypt, until ye came unto this place, ye have been rebellious against the LORD.
Also in {{c1::Horeb}} ye made the LORD wroth, and the LORD was angered with you to have destroyed you.
Also in Horeb ye made the LORD wroth, and the LORD was {{c1::angered}} with you to have destroyed you.

When {{c1::I}} was gone up into the mount to receive the tables of stone, even the tables of the covenant which the LORD made with you, then I abode in the mount forty days and forty nights; I did neither eat bread nor drink water.

When I was gone up into the mount to {{c1::receive}} the tables of stone, even the tables of the covenant which the LORD made with you, then I abode in the mount forty days and forty nights; I did neither eat bread nor drink water.

And the LORD {{c1::delivered}} unto me the two tables of stone written with the finger of God; and on them was written according to all the words, which the LORD spoke with you in the mount out of the midst of the fire in the day of the assembly.

And the LORD delivered unto me the two {{c1::tables}} of stone written with the finger of God; and on them was written according to all the words, which the LORD spoke with you in the mount out of the midst of the fire in the day of the assembly.
And it came to pass at the end of forty days and forty {{c1::nights}}, that the LORD gave me the two tables of stone, even the tables of the covenant.
And it came to pass at the end of forty days and forty nights, that the LORD gave me the two {{c1::tables}} of stone, even the tables of the covenant.

And the LORD said unto me: ‘{{c1::Arise}}, get thee down quickly from hence; for thy people that thou hast brought forth out of Egypt have dealt corruptly; they are quickly turned aside out of the way which I commanded them; they have made them a molten image.’

And the LORD said unto me: ‘Arise, get thee down quickly from hence; for thy people that thou hast brought forth out of {{c1::Egypt}} have dealt corruptly; they are quickly turned aside out of the way which I commanded them; they have made them a molten image.’
Furthermore the LORD spoke unto me, saying: ‘{{c1::I}} have seen this people, and, behold, it is a stiffnecked people;
{{c1::Furthermore}} the LORD spoke unto me, saying: ‘I have seen this people, and, behold, it is a stiffnecked people;
let {{c1::Me}} alone, that I may destroy them, and blot out their name from under heaven; and I will make of thee a nation mightier and greater than they.’
let Me alone, that {{c1::I}} may destroy them, and blot out their name from under heaven; and I will make of thee a nation mightier and greater than they.’
So {{c1::I}} turned and came down from the mount, and the mount burned with fire; and the two tables of the covenant were in my two hands.
So I {{c1::turned}} and came down from the mount, and the mount burned with fire; and the two tables of the covenant were in my two hands.
And {{c1::I}} looked, and, behold, ye had sinned against the LORD your God; ye had made you a molten calf; ye had turned aside quickly out of the way which the LORD had commanded you.
And I {{c1::looked}}, and, behold, ye had sinned against the LORD your God; ye had made you a molten calf; ye had turned aside quickly out of the way which the LORD had commanded you.
And {{c1::I}} took hold of the two tables, and cast them out of my two hands, and broke them before your eyes.
And I took hold of the two {{c1::tables}}, and cast them out of my two hands, and broke them before your eyes.

And {{c1::I}} fell down before the LORD, as at the first, forty days and forty nights; I did neither eat bread nor drink water; because of all your sin which ye sinned, in doing that which was evil in the sight of the LORD, to provoke Him.

And I fell down {{c1::before}} the LORD, as at the first, forty days and forty nights; I did neither eat bread nor drink water; because of all your sin which ye sinned, in doing that which was evil in the sight of the LORD, to provoke Him.
For {{c1::I}} was in dread of the anger and hot displeasure, wherewith the LORD was wroth against you to destroy you. But the LORD hearkened unto me that time also.
For I was in dread of the anger and hot displeasure, wherewith the LORD was wroth against you to destroy you. {{c1::But}} the LORD hearkened unto me that time also.
Moreover the LORD was very angry with {{c1::Aaron}} to have destroyed him; and I prayed for Aaron also the same time.
Moreover the LORD was very angry with Aaron to have destroyed him; and {{c1::I}} prayed for Aaron also the same time.

And {{c1::I}} took your sin, the calf which ye had made, and burnt it with fire, and beat it in pieces, grinding it very small, until it was as fine as dust; and I cast the dust thereof into the brook that descended out of the mount.—

And I took your sin, the calf which ye had made, and burnt it with fire, and beat it in {{c1::pieces}}, grinding it very small, until it was as fine as dust; and I cast the dust thereof into the brook that descended out of the mount.—
And at {{c1::Taberah}}, and at Massah, and at Kibroth-hattaavah, ye made the LORD wroth.
And at Taberah, and at {{c1::Massah}}, and at Kibroth-hattaavah, ye made the LORD wroth.

And when the LORD sent you from {{c1::Kadesh}}-barnea, saying: ‘Go up and possess the land which I have given you’; then ye rebelled against the commandment of the LORD your God, and ye believed Him not, nor hearkened to His voice.

And when the LORD sent you from Kadesh-barnea, saying: ‘{{c1::Go}} up and possess the land which I have given you’; then ye rebelled against the commandment of the LORD your God, and ye believed Him not, nor hearkened to His voice.
Ye have been rebellious against the LORD from the day that {{c1::I}} knew you.—
Ye have been {{c1::rebellious}} against the LORD from the day that I knew you.—
So {{c1::I}} fell down before the LORD the forty days and forty nights that I fell down; because the LORD had said He would destroy you.
So I fell down before the LORD the forty days and forty nights that I fell down; because the LORD had said {{c1::He}} would destroy you.

And {{c1::I}} prayed unto the LORD, and said: ‘O Lord GOD, destroy not Thy people and Thine inheritance, that Thou hast redeemed through Thy greatness, that Thou hast brought forth out of Egypt with a mighty hand.

And I prayed unto the LORD, and said: ‘{{c1::O}} Lord GOD, destroy not Thy people and Thine inheritance, that Thou hast redeemed through Thy greatness, that Thou hast brought forth out of Egypt with a mighty hand.
Remember {{c1::Thy}} servants, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; look not unto the stubbornness of this people, nor to their wickedness, nor to their sin;
Remember Thy servants, {{c1::Abraham}}, Isaac, and Jacob; look not unto the stubbornness of this people, nor to their wickedness, nor to their sin;

lest the land whence {{c1::Thou}} broughtest us out say: Because the LORD was not able to bring them into the land which He promised unto them, and because He hated them, He hath brought them out to slay them in the wilderness.

lest the land whence Thou broughtest us out say: {{c1::Because}} the LORD was not able to bring them into the land which He promised unto them, and because He hated them, He hath brought them out to slay them in the wilderness.
Yet they are {{c1::Thy}} people and Thine inheritance, that Thou didst bring out by Thy great power and by Thy outstretched arm.’
Yet they are Thy people and {{c1::Thine}} inheritance, that Thou didst bring out by Thy great power and by Thy outstretched arm.’
At that time the LORD said unto me: ‘{{c1::Hew}} thee two tables of stone like unto the first, and come up unto Me into the mount; and make thee an ark of wood.
At that time the LORD said unto me: ‘Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the first, and come up unto {{c1::Me}} into the mount; and make thee an ark of wood.
And {{c1::I}} will write on the tables the words that were on the first tables which thou didst break, and thou shalt put them in the ark.’
And I will write on the {{c1::tables}} the words that were on the first tables which thou didst break, and thou shalt put them in the ark.’
So {{c1::I}} made an ark of acacia-wood, and hewed two tables of stone like unto the first, and went up into the mount, having the two tables in my hand.
So I made an ark of {{c1::acacia}}-wood, and hewed two tables of stone like unto the first, and went up into the mount, having the two tables in my hand.

And {{c1::He}} wrote on the tables according to the first writing, the ten words, which the LORD spoke unto you in the mount out of the midst of the fire in the day of the assembly; and the LORD gave them unto me.

And He wrote on the {{c1::tables}} according to the first writing, the ten words, which the LORD spoke unto you in the mount out of the midst of the fire in the day of the assembly; and the LORD gave them unto me.
And {{c1::I}} turned and came down from the mount, and put the tables in the ark which I had made; and there they are, as the LORD commanded me.—
And I {{c1::turned}} and came down from the mount, and put the tables in the ark which I had made; and there they are, as the LORD commanded me.—
And the children of {{c1::Israel}} journeyed from Beeroth-benejaakan to Moserah; there Aaron died, and there he was buried; and Eleazar his son ministered in the priest’s office in his stead.
And the children of Israel journeyed from {{c1::Beeroth}}-benejaakan to Moserah; there Aaron died, and there he was buried; and Eleazar his son ministered in the priest’s office in his stead.
From thence they journeyed unto {{c1::Gudgod}}; and from Gudgod to Jotbah, a land of brooks of water.—
From thence they journeyed unto Gudgod; and from Gudgod to {{c1::Jotbah}}, a land of brooks of water.—
At that time the LORD separated the tribe of {{c1::Levi}}, to bear the ark of the covenant of the LORD, to stand before the LORD to minister unto Him, and to bless in His name, unto this day.
At that time the LORD {{c1::separated}} the tribe of Levi, to bear the ark of the covenant of the LORD, to stand before the LORD to minister unto Him, and to bless in His name, unto this day.
Wherefore {{c1::Levi}} hath no portion nor inheritance with his brethren; the LORD is his inheritance, according as the LORD thy God spoke unto him.—
{{c1::Wherefore}} Levi hath no portion nor inheritance with his brethren; the LORD is his inheritance, according as the LORD thy God spoke unto him.—
Now {{c1::I}} stayed in the mount, as at the first time, forty days and forty nights; and the LORD hearkened unto me that time also; the LORD would not destroy thee.
Now I {{c1::stayed}} in the mount, as at the first time, forty days and forty nights; and the LORD hearkened unto me that time also; the LORD would not destroy thee.
And the LORD said unto me: ‘{{c1::Arise}}, go before the people, causing them to set forward, that they may go in and possess the land, which I swore unto their fathers to give unto them.’
And the LORD said unto me: ‘Arise, go before the people, causing them to set forward, that they may go in and possess the land, which {{c1::I}} swore unto their fathers to give unto them.’

And now, {{c1::Israel}}, what doth the LORD thy God require of thee, but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him, and to serve the LORD thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul;

And now, Israel, what doth the LORD thy God {{c1::require}} of thee, but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him, and to serve the LORD thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul;
to keep for thy good the commandments of the LORD, and His statutes, which {{c1::I}} command thee this day?
to keep for thy good the {{c1::commandments}} of the LORD, and His statutes, which I command thee this day?
{{c1::Behold}}, unto the LORD thy God belongeth the heaven, and the heaven of heavens, the earth, with all that therein is.
Behold, unto the LORD thy God {{c1::belongeth}} the heaven, and the heaven of heavens, the earth, with all that therein is.
Only the LORD had a delight in thy fathers to love them, and {{c1::He}} chose their seed after them, even you, above all peoples, as it is this day.
Only the LORD had a {{c1::delight}} in thy fathers to love them, and He chose their seed after them, even you, above all peoples, as it is this day.
{{c1::Circumcise}} therefore the foreskin of your heart, and be no more stiffnecked.
Circumcise {{c1::therefore}} the foreskin of your heart, and be no more stiffnecked.
For the LORD your God, {{c1::He}} is God of gods, and Lord of lords, the great God, the mighty, and the awful, who regardeth not persons, nor taketh reward.
For the LORD your God, He is God of gods, and Lord of lords, the great God, the {{c1::mighty}}, and the awful, who regardeth not persons, nor taketh reward.
He doth {{c1::execute}} justice for the fatherless and widow, and loveth the stranger, in giving him food and raiment.
He doth execute {{c1::justice}} for the fatherless and widow, and loveth the stranger, in giving him food and raiment.
Love ye therefore the stranger; for ye were strangers in the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.
Love ye {{c1::therefore}} the stranger; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
Thou shalt fear the LORD thy God; Him shalt thou serve; and to Him shalt thou {{c1::cleave}}, and by His name shalt thou swear.
He is thy glory, and He is thy God, that hath done for thee these great and {{c1::tremendous}} things, which thine eyes have seen.
He is thy glory, and He is thy God, that hath done for thee these great and tremendous {{c1::things}}, which thine eyes have seen.
Thy fathers went down into {{c1::Egypt}} with threescore and ten persons; and now the LORD thy God hath made thee as the stars of heaven for multitude.
Thy {{c1::fathers}} went down into Egypt with threescore and ten persons; and now the LORD thy God hath made thee as the stars of heaven for multitude.
{{c1::Therefore}} thou shalt love the LORD thy God, and keep His charge, and His statutes, and His ordinances, and His commandments, alway.
Therefore thou shalt love the LORD thy God, and keep His {{c1::charge}}, and His statutes, and His ordinances, and His commandments, alway.

And know ye this day; for {{c1::I}} speak not with your children that have not known, and that have not seen the chastisement of the LORD your God, His greatness, His mighty hand, and His outstretched arm,

And know ye this day; for I speak not with your {{c1::children}} that have not known, and that have not seen the chastisement of the LORD your God, His greatness, His mighty hand, and His outstretched arm,
and His signs, and His works, which {{c1::He}} did in the midst of Egypt unto Pharaoh the king of Egypt, and unto all his land;
and His signs, and His works, which He did in the midst of {{c1::Egypt}} unto Pharaoh the king of Egypt, and unto all his land;

and what {{c1::He}} did unto the army of Egypt, unto their horses, and to their chariots; how He made the water of the Red Sea to overflow them as they pursued after you, and how the LORD hath destroyed them unto this day;

and what He did unto the army of {{c1::Egypt}}, unto their horses, and to their chariots; how He made the water of the Red Sea to overflow them as they pursued after you, and how the LORD hath destroyed them unto this day;
and what {{c1::He}} did unto you in the wilderness, until ye came unto this place;
and what He did unto you in the {{c1::wilderness}}, until ye came unto this place;

and what {{c1::He}} did unto Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, the son of Reuben; how the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up, and their households, and their tents, and every living substance that followed them, in the midst of all Israel;
